scRNA

aidan borkan

2026-02-21

# installation
#install.packages("Matrix")
#BiocManager::install("GEOquery")
# ensure over 1.1.7
#install.packages("rlang", type = "binary")
#packageVersion("rlang")
reticulate::py_install("umap-learn", pip = TRUE)
## Using virtual environment "C:/Users/suzan/OneDrive - The University of Colorado Denver/Documents/.virtualenvs/r-reticulate" ...
# Comprehensive list
library(GEOquery)
library(dplyr)
library(Seurat)
library(patchwork)
library(data.table)
library(Matrix)
library(enrichplot)
library(stringr)
library(ggplot2)
# Annotations
library(SingleR)
library(celldex)
library(SingleCellExperiment)

# pathway mapping
#install.packages("clusterProfiler")
library(clusterProfiler)
library(org.Hs.eg.db)

gse <- getGEO("GSE75140", GSEMatrix = TRUE)
expr <- exprs(gse[[1]])
# gse is your GEO object (from getGEO)
eset <- gse[[1]]

#expr <- exprs(eset)      
# expression matrix
pheno <- pData(eset)     
# sample metadata
fdata <- fData(eset)     
# feature metadata (genes)
dim(expr)     
## [1]   0 734
# genes x cells 
dim(pheno)
## [1] 734  46
head(colnames(pheno))
## [1] "title"            "geo_accession"    "status"           "submission_date" 
## [5] "last_update_date" "type"

often on GEO we need the supplementary data for expr

path <- "C:/Users/suzan/Downloads/GSE75140_hOrg.fetal.master.data.frame.txt.gz"

# -----------------------------
# 1) Load
# -----------------------------
df <- fread(
  path,
  sep = "\t",
  header = TRUE,
  data.table = FALSE,
  check.names = FALSE
)
cat("Loaded df:", nrow(df), "rows x", ncol(df), "cols\n")
## Loaded df: 734 rows x 18929 cols
# -----------------------------
# 2) Set cell IDs (rownames)
# -----------------------------
id_candidates <- c("cell_id","cell","Cell","barcode","Barcode","cell_name","cellname",
                   "cellID","CellID","cellid","X","...1","V1")
id_col <- intersect(id_candidates, colnames(df))
id_col <- if (length(id_col) >= 1) id_col[1] else colnames(df)[1]

x <- df[[id_col]]
looks_like_id <- (is.character(x) || is.factor(x)) &&
  length(unique(x)) == nrow(df) &&
  !any(is.na(x))

if (looks_like_id) {
  cat("Using cell id column:", id_col, "\n")
  rownames(df) <- as.character(x)
  df[[id_col]] <- NULL
} else {
  cat("No usable cell-id column found; creating Cell_1..Cell_n\n")
  rownames(df) <- paste0("Cell_", seq_len(nrow(df)))
}
## Using cell id column: cell_id
stopifnot(length(rownames(df)) == nrow(df))

# -----------------------------
# 3) Decide expression vs metadata (CORRECT FOR SPARSE scRNA)
#    Key idea: genes are numeric columns. Many genes are low-cardinality because zeros dominate.
#    So DO NOT use "unique > 50" for genes.
# -----------------------------
sample_n <- min(50, nrow(df))
idx <- seq_len(sample_n)

is_mostly_numeric <- vapply(df, function(col) {
  xn <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(col[idx]))
  mean(!is.na(xn)) >= 0.95
}, logical(1))

# Metadata whitelist by NAME (keep this tight)
meta_name_pat <- paste(
  c(
    "^orig\\.ident$",
    "^sample(_id)?$",
    "^donor$",
    "^patient$",
    "^subject$",
    "^batch$",
    "^chip$",
    "^lane$",
    "^run$",
    "^replicate$",
    "^rep$",
    "^time(point)?$",
    "^day$",
    "^week$",
    "^stage$",
    "^tissue$",
    "^region$",
    "^condition$",
    "^group$",
    "^treatment$",
    "^sex$",
    "^age$",
    "GSE",
    "GSM",
    "geo",
    "accession"
  ),
  collapse = "|"
)

is_meta_by_name <- grepl(meta_name_pat, colnames(df), ignore.case = TRUE)

# Expression columns = numeric-ish and NOT named like metadata
expr_cols <- names(df)[is_mostly_numeric & !is_meta_by_name]
meta_cols <- setdiff(names(df), expr_cols)

cat("Expression cols:", length(expr_cols), "\n")
## Expression cols: 18923
cat("Metadata cols:", length(meta_cols), "\n")
## Metadata cols: 5
# sanity: metadata should not be mostly gene symbols
bad_meta_like_genes <- meta_cols[grepl("^[A-Z0-9]+(-[A-Z0-9]+)?$", meta_cols)]
cat("Metadata cols that LOOK like gene symbols (should be small):", length(bad_meta_like_genes), "\n")
## Metadata cols that LOOK like gene symbols (should be small): 4
if (length(bad_meta_like_genes) > 0) print(head(bad_meta_like_genes, 20))
## [1] "GSE1"  "SGSM1" "SGSM2" "SGSM3"
meta_df <- df[, meta_cols, drop = FALSE]
expr_df <- df[, expr_cols, drop = FALSE]

cat("First metadata columns:\n")
## First metadata columns:
print(head(meta_cols, 50))
## [1] "GSE1"    "SGSM1"   "SGSM2"   "SGSM3"   "species"
# -----------------------------
# 4) Convert expression -> numeric matrix (genes x cells)
# -----------------------------
expr_mat_cells_by_genes <- as.matrix(expr_df)
expr_mat_cells_by_genes <- apply(expr_mat_cells_by_genes, 2, function(v) suppressWarnings(as.numeric(v)))
rownames(expr_mat_cells_by_genes) <- rownames(expr_df)

na_frac <- mean(is.na(expr_mat_cells_by_genes))
cat("NA fraction after numeric conversion:", signif(na_frac, 4), "\n")
## NA fraction after numeric conversion: 0
stopifnot(na_frac == 0)

mat <- t(expr_mat_cells_by_genes)  # genes x cells
cat("Expression range:", range(mat), "\n")
## Expression range: 0 14.92125
# -----------------------------
# 5) Build Seurat obj
# -----------------------------
zero_counts <- Matrix(0, nrow = nrow(mat), ncol = ncol(mat), sparse = TRUE)
rownames(zero_counts) <- rownames(mat)
colnames(zero_counts) <- colnames(mat)

obj <- CreateSeuratObject(counts = zero_counts, meta.data = meta_df)
obj <- SetAssayData(obj, assay = "RNA", layer = "data", new.data = mat)

# -----------------------------
# 6) Inspect metadata candidates for sample_id / condition
# -----------------------------
md <- obj@meta.data
cat("Metadata cols in Seurat:", ncol(md), "\n")
## Metadata cols in Seurat: 8
print(colnames(md))
## [1] "orig.ident"   "nCount_RNA"   "nFeature_RNA" "GSE1"         "SGSM1"       
## [6] "SGSM2"        "SGSM3"        "species"
uniq_md <- sort(sapply(md, function(col) length(unique(col))))
cat("\nMetadata columns sorted by #unique values:\n")
## 
## Metadata columns sorted by #unique values:
print(head(uniq_md, 30))
##   nCount_RNA nFeature_RNA      species        SGSM1        SGSM3   orig.ident 
##            1            1            3           38           90           96 
##        SGSM2         GSE1 
##          127          337
# Show frequency tables for columns with smallish number of levels
cand <- names(uniq_md[uniq_md > 1 & uniq_md <= 30])
if (length(cand) == 0) {
  cat("\nNo categorical metadata columns found (uniq between 2 and 30).\n")
} else {
  cat("\nCandidate categorical metadata columns (2..30 uniques):\n")
  print(cand)
  for (nm in head(cand, 10)) {
    cat("\n---", nm, "--- uniques:", uniq_md[[nm]], "\n")
    print(head(table(md[[nm]]), 30))
  }
}
## 
## Candidate categorical metadata columns (2..30 uniques):
## [1] "species"
## 
## --- species --- uniques: 3 
## 
## hESOrg   hOrg  human 
##     96    412    226

differentiate samples

# index of species

obj_sub <- subset(obj, subset = species %in% c("hOrg","human"))
Idents(obj_sub) <- "species"
table(Idents(obj_sub))
## 
##  hOrg human 
##   412   226

inspect distribution to find percentiles that can guide cutoffs

# Add mitochondrial % to metadata
# 1) percent.mt (optional)
# Only makes sense if gene names include MT- genes

# ---- Use data layer for QC proxies ----
data_mat <- GetAssayData(obj, assay = "RNA", layer = "data")

# genes detected proxy (logexpr > 0)
obj$genes_detected <- Matrix::colSums(data_mat > 0)

# library size proxy (sum of logexpr)
obj$sum_logexpr <- Matrix::colSums(data_mat)

# MT signal (robust)
mt_genes <- grep("^MT-", rownames(obj), value = TRUE, ignore.case = TRUE)
if (length(mt_genes) > 0) {
  obj[["percent.mt"]] <- PercentageFeatureSet(obj, pattern = "^MT-", assay = "RNA")
  obj$mt_score <- Matrix::colMeans(data_mat[mt_genes, , drop = FALSE])
} else {
  obj$percent.mt <- NA_real_
  obj$mt_score <- NA_real_
  message("No MT- genes detected; percent.mt and mt_score set to NA.")
}

# ---- HVGs (pick ONE approach) ----
# Option 1 (simple): Seurat HVGs
#  DefaultAssay(obj) <- "RNA"
# obj <- FindVariableFeatures(obj, selection.method = "vst", nfeatures = 2000)

# Option 2 (if you want purely variance on data layer, sparse-safe):
data_mat <- GetAssayData(obj, assay="RNA", layer="data")

gene_means  <- Matrix::rowMeans(data_mat)
gene_meansq <- Matrix::rowMeans(data_mat^2)
gene_var    <- gene_meansq - gene_means^2

VariableFeatures(obj) <- names(sort(gene_var, decreasing=TRUE))[1:2000]

# ---- downstream ----
obj <- ScaleData(obj, features = VariableFeatures(obj))
obj <- RunPCA(obj, features = VariableFeatures(obj))
obj <- RunUMAP(obj, dims = 1:20, umap.method = "uwot", metric = "cosine")
obj <- FindNeighbors(obj, dims = 1:20)
obj <- FindClusters(obj)
## Modularity Optimizer version 1.3.0 by Ludo Waltman and Nees Jan van Eck
## 
## Number of nodes: 734
## Number of edges: 24148
## 
## Running Louvain algorithm...
## Maximum modularity in 10 random starts: 0.7700
## Number of communities: 7
## Elapsed time: 0 seconds
# quick QC summaries
quantile(obj$genes_detected, probs = c(0.01,0.05,0.1,0.5,0.9,0.95,0.99), na.rm = TRUE)
##      1%      5%     10%     50%     90%     95%     99% 
## 1489.09 2086.55 2371.40 3434.00 5612.70 6019.00 6667.09
quantile(obj$sum_logexpr,  probs = c(0.01,0.05,0.1,0.5,0.9,0.95,0.99), na.rm = TRUE)
##        1%        5%       10%       50%       90%       95%       99% 
##  5842.324  9582.974 10818.944 15127.822 22956.413 24089.932 26851.183
if (any(!is.na(obj$mt_score))) {
  quantile(obj$mt_score, probs = c(0.5,0.9,0.95,0.99), na.rm = TRUE)
}

Proxy for standard QC

# we lack real mito
plot_feats <- c("genes_detected", "sum_logexpr")

VlnPlot(obj, features = plot_feats, ncol = length(plot_feats))

# note, geo data matrix does not have mt 
# higher genes_detected means higher transcriptomic complexity

example of how to test if a cluster (3) is biologically meaningful: cluster and plot UMAP and see if 3 is an edge island or randomly scattered fprminmg a diffuse cloud

FeaturePlot(obj, features = c("genes_detected"))

DimPlot(obj, label = TRUE)

follow : https://satijalab.org/seurat/articles/pbmc3k_tutorial

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 3) Scale on HVGs only
# ------------------------------------------------------------

obj <- ScaleData(obj, features = VariableFeatures(obj), vars.to.regress = "sum_logexpr", verbose = FALSE)

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 4) PCA
# ------------------------------------------------------------
obj <- RunPCA(obj, features = VariableFeatures(obj), verbose = FALSE)
ElbowPlot(obj, ndims = 50)

# visually flattens around PC12 - PC15

# pick dims after elbow
nd <- 15

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 5) Neighbors + Clusters
# ------------------------------------------------------------
obj <- FindNeighbors(obj, dims = 1:nd, verbose = FALSE)
obj <- FindClusters(obj, resolution = 0.4, verbose = FALSE)

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 6) UMAP
# ------------------------------------------------------------
obj <- RunUMAP(obj, dims = 1:nd, umap.method = "uwot", metric = "cosine", verbose = FALSE)
DimPlot(obj, reduction = "umap", label = TRUE)

VizDimLoadings(obj, dims = 1:2, reduction = "pca")

DimPlot(obj, reduction = "pca") + NoLegend()

DimHeatmap(obj, dims = 1, cells = 500, balanced = TRUE)

# use dimensions PC1 - PC15

obj <- RunTSNE(obj, dims = 1:15)
DimPlot(obj, reduction = "tsne", label = TRUE)

#let’s find markers

markers <- FindAllMarkers(
  obj,
  only.pos = TRUE,
  min.pct = 0.25,
  logfc.threshold = 0.25
)
## Calculating cluster 0
## For a (much!) faster implementation of the Wilcoxon Rank Sum Test,
## (default method for FindMarkers) please install the presto package
## --------------------------------------------
## install.packages('devtools')
## devtools::install_github('immunogenomics/presto')
## --------------------------------------------
## After installation of presto, Seurat will automatically use the more 
## efficient implementation (no further action necessary).
## This message will be shown once per session
## Calculating cluster 1
## Calculating cluster 2
## Calculating cluster 3
## Calculating cluster 4
# more strict across all rows
markers2 <- markers %>%
  group_by(cluster) %>%
  mutate(p_adj_clusterBH = p.adjust(p_val, method = "BH")) %>%  # explicit per-cluster BH
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(p_adj_globalBH = p.adjust(p_val, method = "BH"))       # global BH across all tests

# sanity: confirm adjusted p-values exist
stopifnot("p_val_adj" %in% colnames(markers))
all.equal(markers2$p_val_adj, markers2$p_adj_clusterBH)
## [1] "Mean relative difference: 0.9975153"
library(dplyr)

# how many markers
markers2 %>%
  summarize(
    n_total = n(),
    n_sig_seurat = sum(p_val_adj < 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),
    n_sig_clusterBH = sum(p_adj_clusterBH < 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),
    n_sig_globalBH = sum(p_adj_globalBH < 0.05, na.rm = TRUE)
  )
## # A tibble: 1 × 4
##   n_total n_sig_seurat n_sig_clusterBH n_sig_globalBH
##     <int>        <int>           <int>          <int>
## 1    5924         3195            5924           5924
# per cluster
markers2 %>%
  group_by(cluster) %>%
  summarize(
    n_tests = n(),
    sig_seurat = sum(p_val_adj < 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),
    sig_globalBH = sum(p_adj_globalBH < 0.05, na.rm = TRUE)
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(sig_seurat))
## # A tibble: 5 × 4
##   cluster n_tests sig_seurat sig_globalBH
##   <fct>     <int>      <int>        <int>
## 1 0          1758       1383         1758
## 2 4          1950        937         1950
## 3 1           979        473          979
## 4 2           780        335          780
## 5 3           457         67          457
top_seurat <- markers2 %>%
  filter(p_val_adj < 0.05) %>%
  group_by(cluster) %>%
  slice_max(order_by = avg_log2FC, n = 10) %>%
  ungroup()

top_global <- markers2 %>%
  filter(p_adj_globalBH < 0.05) %>%
  group_by(cluster) %>%
  slice_max(order_by = avg_log2FC, n = 10) %>%
  ungroup()

key_seurat <- paste(top_seurat$cluster, top_seurat$gene, sep = "::")
key_global <- paste(top_global$cluster, top_global$gene, sep = "::")

overlap_n <- length(intersect(key_seurat, key_global))
only_seurat_n <- length(setdiff(key_seurat, key_global))
only_global_n <- length(setdiff(key_global, key_seurat))

c(overlap = overlap_n, only_seurat = only_seurat_n, only_global = only_global_n)
##     overlap only_seurat only_global 
##          43           7           7
# also which genes dropped out of analysis
dropped <- top_seurat %>%
  mutate(key = paste(cluster, gene, sep="::")) %>%
  filter(!(key %in% key_global)) %>%
  arrange(cluster, desc(avg_log2FC))

dropped
## # A tibble: 7 × 10
##      p_val avg_log2FC pct.1 pct.2 p_val_adj cluster gene  p_adj_clusterBH
##      <dbl>      <dbl> <dbl> <dbl>     <dbl> <fct>   <chr>           <dbl>
## 1 7.52e-34       3.12 0.465 0.076  1.42e-29 0       ILDR2        6.01e-32
## 2 1.03e-20       3.68 0.445 0.133  1.95e-16 2       CA12         2.01e-19
## 3 2.04e-26       2.83 0.71  0.268  3.85e-22 3       NR2F2        9.30e-24
## 4 2.91e- 7       2.78 0.268 0.109  5.51e- 3 3       EBF1         2.83e- 6
## 5 2.67e-11       2.72 0.5   0.242  5.05e- 7 3       LPHN3        1.11e- 9
## 6 2.06e- 9       2.70 0.493 0.253  3.90e- 5 3       NTRK2        4.00e- 8
## 7 5.63e- 8       2.68 0.326 0.143  1.07e- 3 3       TPBG         7.15e- 7
## # ℹ 2 more variables: p_adj_globalBH <dbl>, key <chr>
genes_to_plot <- markers %>%
  filter(!grepl("^RPL|^RPS|^MT-", gene)) %>%
  filter(p_val_adj < 0.05, pct.1 > 0.1) %>%
  group_by(cluster) %>%
  arrange(desc(avg_log2FC), desc(pct.1 - pct.2)) %>%
  slice_head(n = 2) %>%
  pull(gene) %>%
  unique()

## dplyr::select(cluster, gene, avg_log2FC) %>%
  #dplyr::arrange(cluster)

# use for feature plot
FeaturePlot(obj, features = genes_to_plot[1:6], reduction = "umap", order = TRUE)

VlnPlot(obj, features = genes_to_plot[1:6], ncol = 3)

# Investigate cluster 3 specifically
m3 <- FindMarkers(obj, ident.1 = 3, only.pos = TRUE)
head(m3, 15)
##                p_val avg_log2FC pct.1 pct.2    p_val_adj
## NR2F2   2.035873e-26  2.8269893 0.710 0.268 3.852483e-22
## MAB21L1 1.165747e-21  5.4082677 0.261 0.029 2.205943e-17
## RSPO3   6.714057e-18  3.5784261 0.362 0.087 1.270501e-13
## MGAT4C  2.896439e-16  4.7020519 0.210 0.027 5.480931e-12
## SLC41A2 4.581807e-15  2.4725167 0.442 0.159 8.670154e-11
## EBF3    3.862829e-14  6.4681620 0.203 0.032 7.309631e-10
## DDIT3   2.727350e-13  1.5887234 0.652 0.334 5.160964e-09
## DRC1    7.265065e-13  3.1021449 0.174 0.023 1.374768e-08
## INA     1.805417e-12  0.9741592 0.457 0.185 3.416391e-08
## CXXC4   2.343692e-12  1.9300918 0.565 0.272 4.434968e-08
## ADCY2   5.703354e-12  3.2039239 0.283 0.079 1.079246e-07
## SNRPN   1.430710e-11  1.0099152 0.971 0.851 2.707332e-07
## WLS     1.671948e-11  2.2798817 0.529 0.267 3.163827e-07
## PIFO    2.367376e-11  4.2913734 0.225 0.054 4.479786e-07
## LPHN3   2.669590e-11  2.7157339 0.500 0.242 5.051665e-07
obj <- CellCycleScoring(
  obj,
  s.features = cc.genes$s.genes,
  g2m.features = cc.genes$g2m.genes
)
## Warning: The following features are not present in the object: MLF1IP, RPA2,
## not searching for symbol synonyms
VlnPlot(obj, features = c("S.Score", "G2M.Score"))

# let’s make a sample panel to match clusters to most likely cell types

panel <- c("SOX2","PAX6","VIM","NES","HES1","FABP7","SLC1A3",
           "EOMES","DCX","TUBB3",
           "NEUROD6","SATB2","TBR1","SLC17A7",
           "GAD1","GAD2","DLX1","DLX2",
           "GFAP","AQP4","ALDH1L1","S100B",
           "OLIG1","OLIG2","PDGFRA","MBP","PLP1",
           "MKI67","TOP2A","CDK1")

DotPlot(obj, features = panel) + RotatedAxis()

# let’s get a reference

ref <- HumanPrimaryCellAtlasData()

convert Seurat to SingleCellExperiment to use singleR

sce <- as.SingleCellExperiment(obj)

pred_clust <- SingleR(
  test = sce,
  ref = ref,
  labels = ref$label.main,
  clusters = Idents(obj)   # <- key fix
)

pred_clust
## DataFrame with 5 rows and 4 columns
##                           scores               labels delta.next
##                         <matrix>          <character>  <numeric>
## 0 0.692245:0.308609:0.202973:... Neuroepithelial_cell  0.0638150
## 1 0.633299:0.285358:0.170486:... Neuroepithelial_cell  0.1572546
## 2 0.706236:0.316197:0.201729:... Neuroepithelial_cell  0.2057093
## 3 0.686650:0.272004:0.153218:... Neuroepithelial_cell  0.0583742
## 4 0.615353:0.297463:0.208595:...                  MSC  0.0364566
##          pruned.labels
##            <character>
## 0 Neuroepithelial_cell
## 1 Neuroepithelial_cell
## 2 Neuroepithelial_cell
## 3 Neuroepithelial_cell
## 4                  MSC
# 1) Create mapping: cluster -> label (prefer pruned.labels if available)
cluster_to_label <- pred_clust$pruned.labels
cluster_to_label[is.na(cluster_to_label)] <- pred_clust$labels[is.na(cluster_to_label)]
names(cluster_to_label) <- rownames(pred_clust)  # "0"..."6"

# 2) Map to each cell using its cluster id
cell_clusters <- as.character(Idents(obj))        # length = #cells
cell_labels <- cluster_to_label[cell_clusters]
names(cell_labels) <- colnames(obj)              # MUST be cell names

# 3) Store in Seurat metadata
obj <- AddMetaData(obj, metadata = cell_labels, col.name = "SingleR_label")

Idents(obj) <- "seurat_clusters"   # or whatever your clustering column is
table(Idents(obj))
## 
##   0   1   2   3   4 
## 198 184 164 138  50
table(obj$SingleR_label)
## 
##                  MSC Neuroepithelial_cell 
##                   50                  684
table(obj$SingleR_label, obj$seurat_clusters)
##                       
##                          0   1   2   3   4
##   MSC                    0   0   0   0  50
##   Neuroepithelial_cell 198 184 164 138   0

Assign the labels back to Seurat

FeaturePlot(obj, features = c("NEUROD6","RBFOX1","MKI67","SOX2","GFAP"), ncol = 3)

DimPlot(obj, group.by = "SingleR_label", label = TRUE)

# snippet

table(obj$SingleR_label, obj$seurat_clusters)
##                       
##                          0   1   2   3   4
##   MSC                    0   0   0   0  50
##   Neuroepithelial_cell 198 184 164 138   0

convert gene symbols to ENTREZ IDs

sig_genes <- markers %>%
  filter(p_val_adj < 0.05) %>%
  pull(gene) %>%
  unique()

gene_df <- bitr(
  sig_genes,
  fromType = "SYMBOL",
  toType = "ENTREZID",
  OrgDb = org.Hs.eg.db
)
## 'select()' returned 1:1 mapping between keys and columns
## Warning in bitr(sig_genes, fromType = "SYMBOL", toType = "ENTREZID", OrgDb =
## org.Hs.eg.db): 5.52% of input gene IDs are fail to map...
entrez_genes <- gene_df$ENTREZID

Gene ontology enrichement for biological processes

ego <- enrichGO(
  gene = entrez_genes,
  OrgDb = org.Hs.eg.db,
  ont = "BP",            # BP / MF / CC
  pAdjustMethod = "BH",
  pvalueCutoff = 0.05,
  qvalueCutoff = 0.05,
  readable = TRUE
)
# fix spacing and wrap text
ego2 <- ego

ego2@result$Description <- str_wrap(
  ego2@result$Description,
  width = 40
)

dotplot(ego2, showCategory = 15) +
  theme(
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 10),
    plot.margin = margin(10, 30, 10, 10)
  )

barplot(ego2, showCategory = 20)
## Warning in fortify(object, showCategory = showCategory, by = x, ...): Arguments in `...` must be used.
## ✖ Problematic argument:
## • by = x
## ℹ Did you misspell an argument name?

## ℹ Please use tidy evaluation idioms with `aes()`.
## ℹ See also `vignette("ggplot2-in-packages")` for more information.

## generated.

GSEA

# --- 1) Collapse markers to 1 value per gene SYMBOL (avoid duplicates) ---
gene_list <- markers %>%
  dplyr::select(gene, avg_log2FC) %>%
  group_by(gene) %>%
  summarize(avg_log2FC = max(avg_log2FC, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%  # or mean()
  arrange(desc(avg_log2FC))

# --- 2) Map SYMBOL -> ENTREZID (some will fail; that's fine) ---
gene_list_df <- bitr(
  gene_list$gene,
  fromType = "SYMBOL",
  toType = "ENTREZID",
  OrgDb = org.Hs.eg.db
)
## 'select()' returned 1:1 mapping between keys and columns
# --- 3) Join + collapse to 1 value per ENTREZID (avoid 1 SYMBOL -> many ENTREZ) ---
gene_list2 <- gene_list %>%
  inner_join(gene_list_df, by = c("gene" = "SYMBOL")) %>%
  group_by(ENTREZID) %>%
  summarize(avg_log2FC = max(avg_log2FC, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%  # or mean()
  arrange(desc(avg_log2FC))

# --- 4) Named ranking vector (unique names, sorted) ---
gene_vector <- gene_list2$avg_log2FC
names(gene_vector) <- gene_list2$ENTREZID
gene_vector <- sort(gene_vector, decreasing = TRUE)

stopifnot(anyDuplicated(names(gene_vector)) == 0)

# --- 5) Choose scoreType based on sign (pos if all > 0) ---
score_type <- if (all(gene_vector > 0)) "pos" else "std"

# --- RUN GSEA (GO BP) ---
gsea_res <- gseGO(
  geneList = gene_vector,
  OrgDb = org.Hs.eg.db,
  keyType = "ENTREZID",
  ont = "BP",
  pAdjustMethod = "BH",
  scoreType = score_type,
  verbose = FALSE
)

ridge plot of top pathways

ridgeplot(
  gsea_res,
  showCategory = 15
) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 12)
## Picking joint bandwidth of 0.755
